A photography journal

Anand M.

Photographs of light, water and wing, gathered along Karnataka's lakes and balconies.

Scroll

Some evenings the lake turns to foil and a heron forgets to move. These are the frames that stopped to look.

— a running log of stillness

A few that stayed with me

The pieces I keep coming back to — printed, titled, and given a little more room to breathe.

— 02Field Notes — Alkaid

Birds met at the water's edge

Logged one by one, the way a notebook would: who, where, and the light they were standing in.

— 03Night Notes — Seestar S50

What the dark let me see

Same balcony, a smaller aperture, a much longer reach. Logged the way the field notes are — only the distances are measured in lightyears instead of metres.

— 04About

Why I keep a camera nearby

I photograph the quiet edges of Karnataka — lakeshores at first light, a hibiscus on the balcony, birds that hold still just long enough to be noticed. Most days it's just a phone. On clearer mornings, a Seestar S50 does the looking for me.

Nothing here is staged. It's closer to a field notebook than a gallery: a record of what was around, and what the light happened to be doing at the time.

LocationKarnataka, India
GearPhone + Seestar S50
SubjectSpace, Water, Wing, Light
LogOngoing
Bengaluru
© Anand M. — all photographs are his own. Built as a field log, one frame at a time.
